bird.name: 白枕霸鹟
bird.summary: 白枕霸鹟（台湾作白頸異霸鶲，学名：Xenopsaris albinucha），是雀形目南美霸鹟科的一种鸟类，属于单型的白枕霸鹟属（学名：Xenopsaris）。 白枕霸鹟体重约10克，翼长约63.9毫米，喙宽度约3.9毫米，喙厚度约3.9毫米，嘴峰长约10.7毫米，跗蹠长约14…

locations.name: 圣菲省
locations.summary: 圣菲省（西班牙語：Provincia de Santa Fe）為南美國家阿根廷二十三省之一，省会为圣菲。同时也是阿根廷的24个自治政府或一级行政区和24个立法选区之一。。位於阿根廷東北部，是沿海地区（西班牙語：Región del Lit…

locations.name: 阿根廷
locations.summary: 阿根廷共和国（西班牙語：República Argentina），通称阿根廷（西班牙語：Argentina，發音：[aɾxenˈtina] ），是位于南美洲南部的主权国家。由23个省和布宜諾斯艾利斯自治市组成的联邦共和国，占有南錐體的大部…

locations.name: 羅伯特·里奇韋
locations.summary: 羅伯特·里奇韋（英語：Robert Ridgway，1850年7月2日－1929年3月25日），美國鳥類學家，專門研究系統分類學。1881年，史密森學會秘書斯賓塞·富勒頓·貝爾德任命他為美國國家博物館第一位鳥類館館長，該頭銜一直延續到他逝…

locations.name: Fauna of South America
locations.summary: The fauna of South America consists of a huge variety of unique animals some of which evolved in relative isolation.

locations.name: 安地斯山脈
locations.summary: 安地斯山脈（西班牙語：Cordillera de los Andes；奇楚亞語：Walla Antikuna）是地球陸地上最長的山脈，位於南美洲的西岸，約7,000公里長 (或8,900公里长)，200至700公里寬（最寬的部份在南緯18…

locations.name: 南半球
locations.summary: 南半球（英語：Southern Hemisphere）是指地球赤道以南的半球。南半球表面有80.9%是水，佔地球的57.1%；19.1%是陸地，佔地球的32.7%。 南半球包含了地球的五大洲：亚洲印度尼西亞南部、非洲中部及南部、大洋洲絕大…

relations.subject: 白枕霸鹟
relations.evidence: The white-naped xenopsaris (; Xenopsaris albinucha), also known as the reed becard and white-naped becard, is a species of suboscine bird in the family Tityridae, the only member of the genus Xenopsaris

relations.subject: 白枕霸鹟
relations.evidence: It is found in South America, in humid subtropical and tropical savanna climates in most of the countries east of the Andes: Venezuela, Brazil, Bolivia, Paraguay, Uruguay, and Argentina

relations.subject: 白枕霸鹟
relations.evidence: It is found in South America, in humid subtropical and tropical savanna climates in most of the countries east of the Andes: Venezuela, Brazil, Bolivia, Paraguay, Uruguay, and Argentina

relations.subject: 白枕霸鹟
relations.evidence: The subspecies X. a. minor is found in west and central Venezuela, and probably extends into north-eastern Colombia. The species is generally resident across its range, but sightings of solitary and silent birds have suggested that the species may be migratory in Bolivia and Brazil. A study published in 2005 suggested it was migratory in Santa Fe, Argentina, as the species was not observed in the area between March and September (the austral winter). In 2006 the species was reported for the first time in Peru, but it was unclear if this represented a vagrant escaping cold weather or a migrant, as the species is mostly uncommon across its range and that area is poorly studied ornithologically. They occupy a variety of habitats across their range, including Caatinga scrubland, riparian (river) woodland, lightly wooded areas, the borders of open gallery forest and open areas with scattered trees
